title: Task management
journey: Right now, a school's work-to-do lives scattered across email, group chats, spreadsheets, and half-remembered conversations after a meeting. Task management gives every piece of work — from a quick reminder to a whole-team responsibility — one canonical record: who owns it, what state it's in, when it's due, and a real audit trail of what changed and why. The goal is that 'what's outstanding, and whose is it' is always a real answer, not a guess.

subfeatures.name: Canonical task record
subfeatures.note: The core fields every task has, and how they're stored — the foundation everything else builds on.

subfeatures.name: Status changes & overdue behaviour
subfeatures.note: Marking work done, tracking what's overdue, and keeping that in sync with dashboards.

subfeatures.name: Owner & group assignment
subfeatures.note: Assigning work to a person or a whole group, without losing individual accountability.

subfeatures.name: Dashboard visibility
subfeatures.note: Surfacing upcoming, overdue, and high-priority work automatically.

subfeatures.name: Workflow-generated tasks
subfeatures.note: A workflow template automatically creates the right tasks when it's launched.

subfeatures.name: Audit history
subfeatures.note: A record of every important change to a task, for accountability.

subfeatures.name: Contribution tracking
subfeatures.note: Tracking who did what within a task assigned to a group.

subfeatures.name: Private task visibility
subfeatures.note: Making sure a task marked private actually stays private — enforced by the database, not just the interface.

testing.fixtureNote: Tested against a shared fixture organisation of 10 members, of whom 2 (a principal-style owner account and a non-assignable IT admin) can never hold a task — so 'assign to everyone' must always offer exactly 8 people. The same fixture covers every way a task can be assigned (directly, by role, by registry, by group, by responsibility post) and every task state, including a private task that must stay invisible to everyone but its owner.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: The assignee picker includes the right people
expectedOutcomes.expected: The pool includes the current user plus other people and groups, in both the quick-preview mode and the real signed-in mode — with the same filtering rules applied either way.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: "Assign to all members" offers exactly 8 people
expectedOutcomes.expected: Never the two accounts the fixture marks as non-assignable.
expectedOutcomes.enforcedBy: known from the fixture; not yet an automated test

expectedOutcomes.criterion: A task assigned to a whole group reaches every member
expectedOutcomes.expected: One assignment entry is created per member. The signed-in system resolves this on the server; the quick-preview mode approximates it in the browser.
